top_weaknesses: Soother PDP screenshot shows a 'Sorry, something went wrong' error message where a transaction module likely belongs — the report frames buy-box absence as purely a content gap and misses this as possible evidence the WooCommerce buy module is broken site-wide (same root cause as the cart shortcode), which would sharpen exp-a1c7f0e94b22 and the diagnosis.

AOV/retention experiments (sampler, kits, subscribe, routine) rest on the catalog but are partly speculative product-creation bets rather than tested-from-evidence gaps; lift/confidence figures are plausible but uncalibrated.
Several decision rules are slightly soft ('improves without hurting...') without explicit magnitude thresholds or sample/duration guardrails.
Several AOV/retention experiments (sampler, kit, subscribe) are reasonable but lean on inference rather than directly evidenced leaks; lift figures are unverifiable estimates.

Some uncertainty about whether the store transacts directly (WooCommerce shortcode suggests it does, but PDPs show no buy box even in working captures) — the report hedges but never fully resolves the retailer-routed vs. direct-sell question.
Competitor 'pattern to adapt' rows are solid but a couple (e.g., Chimes flavor sampler) are slightly generic restatements of the proposed experiments.

Site appears to be a brand/retailer-routed presence (no transactional buy box anywhere, WooCommerce cart likely deprecated); several conversion/AOV/retention experiments assume direct e-commerce that may not exist, making lift estimates and 'ship a sampler PDP' steps potentially infeasible
Lift/confidence figures are not strongly justified per-experiment; KPIs like 60-day repeat rate require direct-commerce data that the brand may not capture
